lede: A look at May 16 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Henry Fonda and IU (entertainer), and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Gikomba bombings
events.desc: Twelve people are killed in two explosions in the Gikomba market area of Nairobi, Kenya.

events.title: Junko Tabei
events.desc: Junko Tabei from Japan becomes the first woman to reach the summit of Mount Everest.

events.title: Park Chung Hee
events.desc: Park Chung Hee leads a coup d'état to overthrow the Second Republic of South Korea.

events.title: Theodore Maiman
events.desc: Theodore Maiman operates the first optical laser (a ruby laser), at Hughes Research Laboratories in Malibu, California.

events.title: Tritons' Fountain
events.desc: The Tritons' Fountain in Valletta, Malta is turned on for the first time.

events.title: Claudio Monteverdi
events.desc: The first modern performance of Claudio Monteverdi's opera Il ritorno d'Ulisse in patria occurred in Paris.

events.title: Sedition Act of 1918
events.desc: The Sedition Act of 1918 is passed by the U.S. Congress, making criticism of the government during wartime an imprisonable offense. It will be repealed less than two years later.

events.title: Juan Godoy
events.desc: Juan Godoy discovers the rich silver outcrops of Chañarcillo sparking the Chilean silver rush.

events.title: Peninsular War
events.desc: Peninsular War: The allies Spain, Portugal and United Kingdom fight an inconclusive battle against the French at the Albuera. It is, in proportion to the numbers involved, the bloodiest battle of the war.

events.title: Mohnyin Thado
events.desc: Gov. Thado of Mohnyin becomes King of Ava.

observances.text: Christian feast day: Abda and Abdjesus, and companions: Abdas of Susa
Christian feast day: Brendan the Navigator (Roman Catholic Church, Anglican Communion, Eastern Orthodox Church)
Christian feast day: Caroline Chisholm (Church of England)
